content.problemStatement:
Chef played an interesting game yesterday. This game is played with two variables XXX and YYY; initially, X=Y=0X = Y = 0X=Y=0. Chef may make an arbitrary number of moves (including zero). In each move, he must perform the following process:


Choose any positive integer PPP such that P⋅P>YP \cdot P \gt YP⋅P>Y.
Change XXX to PPP.
Add P⋅PP \cdot PP⋅P to YYY.
Unfortunately, Chef has a bad memory and he has forgotten the moves he made. He only remembers the value of XXX after the game finished; let's denote it by XfX_fXf​. Can you tell him the maximum possible number of moves he could have made in the game?
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer XfX_fXf​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum number of moves Chef could have made.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 2: One possible sequence of values of XXX is 0→1→2→3→5→80 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow 5 \rightarrow 80→1→2→3→5→8.
